title: polypodium exosum
intro: Polypodium exosum, also known as Devil's Claw, is a type of flowering plant native to the Kalahari Desert in southern Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which are attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides, specifically harpagoside. The medicinal properties of Polypodium exosum have been found to provide various health benefits, including relief from arthritis, muscle and joint pain, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of capsules, tablets, or teas, and is also used topically as a poultice or ointment.
